questions: What can you show or tell about speak in a full sentence?

No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
No decorative empty balloons or long writing.

compiled_prompt:
# BCube Production Prompt v3 — CC-LKG-V3-P009

## 1. Release metadata

- Prompt ID: CC-LKG-V3-P009
- Version: 3.0.0
- Book: Communication Champions
- Level: LKG (4+)
- Module: Module 1 – Listening and Speaking
- Page: 9 of 41
- Exact title: Speak in a Full Sentence
- Source: Communication_Champions_LKG_Gold_Production_v2_Final.xlsx
- Source SHA-256: f9b5c4bbf026f68f6f46a9975b0407ca33b3e2351d2ecb92dcaa85518d7c97a3

## 2. Production command

Create exactly one final, flat, front-facing A4 portrait page for “Speak in a Full Sentence”. Follow this deterministic package only. Produce no explanation, alternate, mockup, or additional page.

## 3. Engine order and precedence

1. Publishing Engine
2. Design Engine
3. Visual Grammar Engine
4. Educational Engine
5. Teaching Engine
6. Parent Partnership Engine
7. Illustration Engine
8. Character Engine
9. Quality Assurance and Prompt Compiler

Precedence: approved page instruction > approved book/level rule > global rule. Do not resolve conflicts by guessing.

## 4. Publishing Engine

- A4 portrait, 210 × 297 mm; 3 mm bleed; minimum 10 mm safe margin; 12 mm binding margin; 300 DPI; CMYK-safe; print-ready.
- Use a 20–28 mm header with the exact title “Speak in a Full Sentence” centred and the official BCube logo reserved at top left.
- Use a 10–14 mm footer with page number 9 only at bottom right.
- Never generate or redraw the logo; apply the official vector asset during final layout.

## 5. Design and Visual Grammar Engines

- Page type: activity_page. Layout anchor: Picture-supported speaking.
- Visual/text balance: 60–70% visual and 30–40% text/activity. Use one dominant focal point and a clear title → model → action → response eye path.
- Blank-space rule: One compact sentence strip only. Maximum two core activities and one optional extension.
- Clean white base, soft BCube pastel accents, thick rounded outlines, large targets, strong contrast, generous spacing, and no visual clutter.

## 6. Educational Engine

- Objective: Expand one-word answers into short sentences.
- Primary activity: Complete one model-and-repeat or point-and-say task for “Speak in a Full Sentence”.
- Secondary activity: Use one picture-supported sentence starter.
- Observable evidence: One exact 3–8 word sentence.
- Developmental response modes: oral response, guided tracing, short-word copying, sequencing, drawing, matching, or one supported sentence frame.

## 7. Teaching Engine

- Facilitation: Model the phrase slowly and invite repetition.
- Model once, invite participation, pause for processing, scaffold through gesture/choice/model language, and affirm effort without shame.
- Teacher–student interaction must be visible, purposeful, inclusive, and connected to the exact learning objective.
- Assessment evidence: Child independently completes the activity and verbally explains the learning.

## 8. Parent Partnership Engine

- Home connection: Repeat learning at home using everyday situations.
- Use common household materials only; do not create a new scored task or require a device, purchase, or printing.

## 9. Illustration and Character Engines

- Composition: Follow the individual blueprint exactly.
- Show natural expressions, correct anatomy, inclusive children and families, clear turn-taking, unobstructed activity areas, and editable visible text.
- Star uses the locked bright-yellow rounded five-point identity, expressive face, blue shoes, and small blue cape; Star demonstrates or encourages without completing the child’s answer.

## 10. Approved source instruction

TECHNICAL AND PUBLISHING SPECIFICATION: Create an A4 portrait educational workbook page, 210 × 297 mm, 300 DPI, print-ready, with a clean white background, pastel accents, thick rounded black outlines, generous safe margins, and a premium preschool publishing finish. Use the official BCube logo at top left, exact page title centred at the top, and page number at bottom right. Do not show the LKG (4+) badge on interior pages. LKG DEVELOPMENT RULES: Designed for children aged approximately 4–5. Use familiar vocabulary, short model sentences, picture-supported instructions, guided tracing or copying, and one clear communication focus. Use a maximum of two core activities and one optional extension. TEACHING CLARITY RULES: Every page must show the exact child action, a short teacher or parent prompt, the expected child response, and purposeful response space. Do not use decorative blank balloons or unexplained boxes. Every visible frame, line, card, and balloon must have a learning purpose. PAGE-SPECIFIC ART DIRECTION: Exact title “Speak in a Full Sentence”, page number 9. Show four picture cards: dog, red ball, girl running, boy reading. Provide model frames “It is a ______.” and “The ______ is ______.” INDIVIDUAL PAGE EXECUTION BLUEPRINT: Primary activity: Complete one model-and-repeat or point-and-say task for “Speak in a Full Sentence”. Secondary task: Use one picture-supported sentence starter. Teacher prompt: Model the phrase slowly and invite repetition. Expected child response: One exact 3–8 word sentence. Blank-space allocation: One compact sentence strip only. Activity load: 2 activities. Do not add: No decorative empty balloons or long writing. OFFICIAL STAR MASCOT: Use one consistent bright yellow five-point Star with softly rounded points, large expressive eyes, rosy cheeks, friendly smile, blue shoes, and a small blue cape. Keep Star outside all response areas. QUALITY RULES: No watermark, unrelated logo, photorealism, gibberish text, distorted anatomy, extra fingers, crowded layouts, long passages, unsafe scenes, or blocked activity spaces. Use British English consistently.

## 11. Negative constraints

- No generated or redrawn BCube logo; reserve the approved position for the official vector asset.
- No unapproved text, labels, tasks, decorative frames, empty balloons, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
- No unexplained blank areas; every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area serves a named learning action.
- No frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, unsafe behaviour, inaccessible instructions, anatomy errors, cropped key elements, or developmentally excessive writing.
- No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
- No decorative empty balloons or long writing.

## 12. Gold QA gate and compiler footer

Verify page identity, geometry, bleed, safe zones, logo reservation, exact title, objective alignment, activity load, teacher interaction, parent extension, expected response, character continuity, inclusion, print suitability, prohibited extras, and unresolved variables. Any critical defect blocks release.

CC-LKG-V3-P009 | v3.0.0 | page 9/41 | Gold 100/100 | critical defects 0 | GOLD CERTIFIED.
